pr.title: Clean some ascii and pointers in .data
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Converted a small region of asm/data.s from opaque hexadecimal .4byte string data and raw address constants into readable .asciz strings, .balign 4 padding, explicit labels, and symbolic label references. The cleanup covered toy stand / toy figure asset strings around lbl_803FDDC4 and visual scene asset strings around lbl_804000D0. There were no PR comments or review notes in the provided slice.
what_changed: Replaced hexadecimal .4byte encodings of ASCII strings with readable .asciz directives for asset and joint names.
Added .balign 4 after converted strings to preserve word alignment previously implied by 4-byte chunks and zero padding.
Introduced explicit global labels for string locations such as lbl_803FDE84, lbl_803FDEA4, lbl_803FDED4, lbl_803FDEF4, lbl_803FDF18, and lbl_803FDF48.
Replaced raw address constants such as 0x803FDE84, 0x803FDEA4, 0x803FDED4, 0x803FDEF4, 0x803FDF18, and 0x803FDF48 with symbolic .4byte label references.

Converted strings including TyStand.dat, ToyStandModel_TopN_joint, ToyFigurePanel_Top_joint, ToyFigureBack_Top_joint, ToyFigurePanel_Top_animjoint, ToyFigurePanel_Top_matanim_joint, ToyFigurePanel_Top_shapeanim_joint, ToyFigureBack_Top_animjoint, ToyFigureBack_Top_matanim_joint, ToyFigureBack_Top_shapeanim_joint, Vi0502.dat, visual0502Scene, and IrAls.dat.
smart_moves: Used .asciz rather than hand-written packed .4byte words, making asset names directly searchable and less error-prone.
Inserted .balign 4 after each converted string so subsequent pointer/data layout remains aligned like the original word-based representation.
Promoted internal string offsets to labels before using them as pointer targets, avoiding brittle raw absolute addresses in .data.
Kept existing unknown lbl_* names where semantic names were not established, which improves readability without inventing unverified symbol names.
decomp_lessons: When packed ASCII in .data is clearly null-terminated text, converting it to .asciz improves maintainability while preserving binary layout if alignment is handled explicitly.
Raw .4byte absolute addresses inside data tables should be replaced with .4byte label references once the target location is identifiable.
Adding labels at pointer targets can document embedded string/table boundaries and make future struct recovery easier.
For data cleanup PRs, verify that .asciz length plus .balign 4 exactly matches the old sequence of packed words and null padding.
naming_conventions: Preserved autogenerated address labels in the lbl_80xxxxxx style for unnamed data symbols.
Asset filenames and joint names were kept with their original capitalization, including TyStand.dat, Vi0502.dat, IrAls.dat, ToyFigurePanel_Top_joint, and ToyFigureBack_Top_matanim_joint.
No new semantic C-style symbol names were introduced in the provided diff; the cleanup stayed conservative.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use .asciz "..." for embedded null-terminated asset strings instead of hexadecimal .4byte ASCII chunks.
Follow converted strings with .balign 4 to maintain data section alignment and matching offsets.
Replace numeric pointer literals with symbolic label references in .4byte directives.
Create .global label declarations at embedded targets when nearby data tables reference those addresses.
searchable_terms: Clean some ascii and pointers in .data
follow_up_queries: Find other asm/data.s regions where ASCII is still encoded as hexadecimal .4byte values and can be safely converted to .asciz plus .balign 4.
Search for raw 0x803Fxxxx or 0x8040xxxx pointer constants in .data that could be replaced with labels.
Investigate whether toy figure and visual scene data tables around lbl_803FDDC4 and lbl_804000D0 can be promoted from raw asm data into named structs or C data declarations.
